title: DerivativeRegister
description:
POST /derivatives/register — the on-device AI contribute-back (OL-1). A consumer that computed expensive, identity-bearing AI on-device (Apple FeaturePrint/Vision embeddings, faces, OCR, Speech transcripts, auto-tags, aesthetics — the LoupeJSON `ai` block) FIRST writes the blob through the mount to <nas_root>/.juicemount/derivatives/<inode>/ai.loupe.json (it has write access), THEN calls this to make the server index it so every other client + the web UI reuses it. AI-ONLY (kind:"ai"); tech/thumbnail/filmstrip/waveform/proxy are the server lane's job and never pushed back. The server is the source-hash authority: the consumer cannot compute the contract xxh3 (it uses xxh64), so it VOUCHES with source_size+source_mtime (the live stat it computed the AI against); the server re-stats and accepts ONLY on a size+mtime match (else 409 — the AI was computed against stale bytes), then stamps the canonical xxh3 itself.

REQUEST body (application/json): { inode:int, kind:"ai", producer:"on-device", model:string, dim:int, source_size:int, source_mtime:int, blob_rel_path:"ai.loupe.json" }.

This document is the 200 RESPONSE.

inode.description: Echo of the registered asset's JuiceFS inode.

registered.description: true on a successful register (200). A stale-bytes register returns HTTP 409 with no body validated here.

derivative.description: The manifest row the server wrote — identical shape to a derivatives.schema.json entry. The server stamped source_hash (its own xxh3), updated_at, and status:ready.

producer.description: Who computed it. on-device = Apple frameworks / BYO cloud key on a Mac.

hash.description: The server's canonical xxh3 of the source (== source_hash). On-device consumers verify with size/mtime, not this; an xxh3-capable client/web UI can verify by hash.

blob_rel_path.description: Volume-relative under /.juicemount/derivatives/<inode>/. The consumer wrote the bytes; the server only indexes the row.

model.description: Embedding model id+version so kNN partitions by space (e.g. apple-featureprint-v1 vs the portable openclip-vit-b32-v1).
